questions.question: An autoencoder's bottleneck layer forces the model to:
options: Learn a compressed latent representation of the input
questions.explanation: The bottleneck has fewer dimensions than input — the encoder must learn to preserve the most important information.

questions.question: Denoising autoencoders are trained to:
options: Reconstruct clean input from noisy input — learning robust features
questions.explanation: Input: noisy x. Target: clean x. The model learns features that are robust to noise.

questions.question: A Variational Autoencoder (VAE) differs from a standard autoencoder by:
options: Encoding inputs as probability distributions, enabling smooth sampling
questions.explanation: VAE encoder outputs (mu, sigma) defining a Gaussian. Sampling from this space allows generation of new realistic examples.

questions.question: In a GAN, the discriminator is trained to:
options: Distinguish real samples from generator fakes
questions.explanation: D outputs P(real). Trained with real=1, fake=0 labels. G tries to fool D into outputting 1 for generated samples.

questions.question: Mode collapse in GAN training means:
options: The generator produces limited variety, ignoring most of the data distribution
Training is unstable only at the start
questions.explanation: G finds a small set of outputs that consistently fool D, ignoring diversity. The generated samples look similar to each other.

questions.question: The Wasserstein GAN (WGAN) improves training stability by:
options: Replacing the JS-divergence with Wasserstein distance as the GAN loss
questions.explanation: Wasserstein distance provides meaningful gradients even when real and fake distributions don't overlap — avoiding mode collapse.

questions.question: Cycle-GAN is used for:
options: Unpaired image-to-image translation (e.g. horse ↔ zebra) without paired data
questions.explanation: CycleGAN trains two generators (A→B and B→A) with cycle-consistency loss — no paired examples needed.

questions.question: A high reconstruction error in a trained autoencoder indicates:
options: An anomaly — the model cannot reconstruct unusual inputs
questions.explanation: Autoencoders trained on normal data encode normal patterns well. Anomalies have high reconstruction error — a free anomaly detector.

questions.question: The latent space of a VAE is:
options: Continuous and structured — nearby points decode to similar outputs
questions.explanation: The KL divergence term in VAE loss regularises the latent space to be continuous and approximately Gaussian.

questions.question: StyleGAN controls image generation at different levels by:
options: Injecting style vectors at each layer — coarse (pose) vs fine (colour) control
questions.explanation: StyleGAN's mapping network converts latent z to styles w, injected at each resolution — enabling disentangled control.
